mechanical skills (bench, lath: e multilingual (english, milling, welding) hindi, malayalam) projects event based cameras for fluid flow measurement 09/2023-present e demonstrated research abilities and critical appraisal skills through undergraduate dissertation on the title event-based cameras for fluid flow measurement and produced a 16000-word report. e planned and conducted piv experiments to measure velocity components using both event-based and piv cameras. e developed matlab code to convert event data into image frames for cross-correlation analysis on pivlab software. e performed comparative analysis on the efficiency of both camera types, highlighting advantages and disadvantages, with findings showing only a maximum discrepancy of 26% in fluid velocity magnitudes. hyperloop manchester 09/2022-present e attained the role of magnetic braking engineer for the hyperloop society at the university of manchester by showcasing proficiency in presentation skills and subject matter knowledge during q&a session. e designed a circular spinning track and magnet engaging system to calculate braking force of the halbach array to validate theoretical results. e designed experiments which showcased expertise in safety and reliability engineering debris detecting satellite 09/2023-12/2023 e appointed as the mechanical engineer in a 9-student project, completing a first-class group report that consists of the conceptual design of a satellite system up to the preliminary design review (pdr) phase. implemented matlab to create two separate codes to produce the satellite structural and thermal model. e performed the overall satellite mass and cost estimation, balancing material properties and budget constraints to achieve a feasible and robust design for the satellite structure. e demonstrated skills in poster making, presentation and ability to pitch satellite design to aerospace professors. giant foamboard aircraft 09/2022-07/2023 e selected as the
